title: GatewaySend destination `onCall` ignores `transferFrom` result and continues as if funds were received
reason: Duplicate of existing F-005, which already covers untrusted payload accounting plus ignored ERC20 transferFrom result in `GatewaySend.onCall`.

title: Unbounded receiver bytes are persisted in refund records, enabling revert-callback gas exhaustion
reason: Not reportable as stated: upstream gateway enforces message/revert size caps, and no concrete protocol-wide gas-exhaustion path was substantiated.

title: GatewayTransferNative exposes an ETH-sentinel path in `withdrawToNativeChain` that reverts at fee transfer
reason: Subset/variant of existing F-004 (underfunded sentinel path draining reserves); separate liveness framing is not distinct root cause.

title: Missing deadline validation in GatewaySend swap execution
reason: `_doMixSwap` forwards deadline to DODO router, which is expected to enforce it; no independent deadline bypass was demonstrated in this code.

title: No minimum output amount enforcement on source-chain swaps
reason: `minReturnAmount` is part of user-supplied swap params and enforced by the downstream swap; absence of extra wrapper checks is not a standalone vulnerability.

title: Out-of-bounds calldata read in decodePackedMessage
reason: Malformed length fields cause a revert on that call path rather than exploitable memory corruption or cross-user fund impact.

title: Predictable externalId enables front-running on source chains
reason: `externalId` is an identifier/event key, not an authorization primitive; predictability does not create direct theft or protocol manipulation by itself.

title: Gas stipend limitation in refund ETH transfers
reason: Factually incorrect: `TransferHelper.safeTransferETH` uses `.call` without a 2300-gas stipend limit.

title: Missing access control on GatewayTransferNative.withdraw function
reason: Already captured by existing F-008, where exploitability depends on residual gateway allowances and contract balances.

title: Platform fee can be set to zero enabling free withdrawals
reason: Governance/owner-configurable economic parameter, not a security flaw in permissioning or accounting.

title: Incorrect amountIn calculation in swap finalization
reason: The cited check is conservative and may only over-revert in edge cases; no realistic theft, insolvency, or permissionless DoS exploit was shown.
